hero.title: PDF Splitter
hero.tagline: Just Drop and Go — Extract the pages you need from any PDF by entering simple page ranges (e.g. 1, 3-5, 7-10). Get each range as its own file, or merge them all into one combined PDF. No software needed — upload, split, and download in seconds.

hint.text: Do you need to merge PDFs?

whatIs.heading: What is PDF splitting
whatIs.body: PDF splitting is the operation of extracting a subset of pages from a source PDF and writing them into one or more output documents. The operation is non-destructive — the source file is not modified — and produces output PDFs that share the same media boxes, fonts, and embedded resources as the original pages.

outputOptions.heading: Output options
outputOptions.intro: Two output modes are supported, letting you choose the right result for your workflow:
outputOptions.multiple: Multiple files: each page range produces an independent PDF.
outputOptions.single: Single combined file: all selected ranges are assembled into one PDF in the order they appear.

pageRangeFormat.heading: Page range format
pageRangeFormat.intro: Ranges are entered as a comma-separated list. The supported syntax is:
pageRangeFormat.note: Ranges are inclusive and 1-indexed. The tool validates input and reports errors for out-of-range or malformed values.

tradeoffs.heading: Design tradeoffs
tradeoffs.fidelity: Fidelity: Page content is reproduced exactly; no recompression or re-encoding of resources.
tradeoffs.performance: Performance: Processing time scales with file size and number of selected pages.
tradeoffs.order: Order: Pages appear in the output in the order defined by the range expression.

practical.heading: Practical recommendations
practical.item1: For recurring extractions from a standard document, note the page numbers in advance.
practical.item2: If you only need a few pages from a large file, splitting first reduces downstream processing time.
practical.item3: Combine splitting and merging when you need to reorder or selectively combine pages from multiple documents.

whatItDoes.heading: What it does
whatItDoes.item1: Extracts one or more page ranges from a PDF.
whatItDoes.item2: Produces one output file per range, or a single combined file.
whatItDoes.item3: Preserves original page content and embedded resources.
whatItDoes.item4: Runs server-side for reliable handling of large files.

usefulWhen.heading: Useful when
usefulWhen.item1: You need to share only specific chapters or sections of a document.
usefulWhen.item2: You want to split a scanned multi-page document into individual files.
usefulWhen.item3: You need to extract appendices or exhibits from a report.
usefulWhen.item4: You need to reduce file size by removing unneeded pages before sharing or archiving.

comparison.heading: Comparison with alternatives
comparison.browser: Browser-based: convenient, no installation; suitable for occasional use.
comparison.desktop: Desktop tools: better suited for batch workflows or documents requiring special handling.
comparison.commandLine: Command-line tools: fastest for automation and scripted workflows.

privacy.heading: Privacy & retention
privacy.body: Uploaded files are processed on short-lived infrastructure and removed according to the app's retention policy. If you are handling highly sensitive documents, prefer local desktop tools or an on-prem solution that matches your compliance requirements.

faq.heading: FAQs
faq.a1: A: Files are processed temporarily and auto-deleted according to the app's policy.
faq.q2: Q: Can I extract non-contiguous pages?
faq.a2: A: Yes — use a comma-separated list such as 1,3,7.
faq.q3: Q: What happens to the original PDF?
faq.a3: A: The original is not modified; only the selected pages are written to new output files.
faq.q4: Q: Can I combine the split ranges into one file?
faq.a4: A: Yes — select the 'Single' output option to receive all ranges merged into one file.
faq.q5: Q: What if my range is out of bounds?
faq.a5: A: The tool validates your input and shows an error before attempting to process.

howItWorks.step1: Upload a PDF using drag & drop or the browse button.
howItWorks.step2: Enter the page ranges you want (e.g. 1,3-5,7-10) and choose Single or Multiple output.
howItWorks.step3: Click Split PDF — the file is uploaded to R2 storage and the splitter processes your ranges.
howItWorks.step4: Download each segment (or the combined file) before it expires from temporary storage.

guide.title: How to Split PDF Files Efficiently (Take Control of Large Documents)
guide.intro: Big PDF files can be overwhelming — dozens of pages, mixed content you don't need, sections that should really be separate. Instead of working with the entire file, you can split it into smaller, focused documents.
guide.cta: 👉 In this guide, you'll learn how to break PDFs into exactly what you need — quickly and cleanly.

whatIs.heading: 📄 What Is a PDF Splitter?
whatIs.body: A PDF splitter is a tool that lets you:
whatIs.item2: 📑 Separate a large file into multiple PDFs
whatIs.item3: 🎯 Keep only the content you need

whyUseful.heading: 🧠 Why Splitting PDFs Is Useful
whyUseful.item1: 🎯 Focus on Relevant Content — keep only the pages you need instead of scrolling through everything.
whyUseful.item2: 📤 Share Specific Sections — no need to send a 50-page document when only 3 pages matter.
whyUseful.item3: ⚡ Improve Workflow Efficiency — smaller files are faster to open, easier to manage, and quicker to upload.
whyUseful.item4: 📂 Better Organization — turn one large file into multiple categorized documents.

useCases.heading: 🛠 Common Use Cases
useCases.item1: 💼 Business Documents — extract contracts, invoices, reports
useCases.item2: 🎓 Academic Work — separate chapters or submit only required pages
useCases.item3: 📑 Legal or Official Files — isolate important pages, keep records organized
useCases.item4: 🧾 Scanned Documents — split scanned pages into individual files

ways.heading: 🔍 Ways to Split a PDF
ways.item1: 📌 By Page Range — e.g. Pages 1–5 → File A, Pages 6–10 → File B
ways.item2: 📌 Extract Specific Pages — pick exact pages (Page 2, 5, 9 → new file)
ways.item3: 📌 Split Every Page — turn one PDF into multiple single-page files

stepByStep.heading: 🪜 Step-by-Step: How to Split a PDF

bestPractices.heading: 🎯 Best Practices for Splitting PDFs
bestPractices.item1: ✅ Plan before splitting — know what pages you need and how you want them grouped.
bestPractices.item2: ✅ Keep file names clear — name files logically (e.g., "Chapter1.pdf").
bestPractices.item3: ✅ Avoid over-splitting — too many small files can become hard to manage.
bestPractices.item4: ✅ Check output files — verify correct pages and proper order.
bestPractices.item5: ✅ Keep the original file — in case you need additional pages later.

proTips.heading: 🚀 Pro Tips for Better Workflow
proTips.item4: 📌 Combine with merging for full control

safety.heading: 🔐 Is It Safe to Split PDFs Online?
safety.body: Most modern tools process files temporarily and remove them after download. Still, avoid uploading sensitive documents unless you trust the tool.

faq.heading: ❓ FAQ
faq.q1: Can I split a PDF without losing quality?
faq.a1: Yes — splitting does not affect content quality.
faq.q2: Can I extract just one page?
faq.a2: Absolutely — you can extract any page individually.
faq.q3: Is there a limit to file size?
faq.a3: Depends on the tool, but most support reasonably large files.
faq.a4: No — but you can merge files back together if needed.

guide.conclusion: Splitting PDFs gives you control over your documents. Instead of dealing with large, cluttered files, you can extract only what matters, organize content efficiently, and work faster and smarter.
